---
title: "Quotient vector spaces"
description: "A first sample math note"
categories: [math, linear algebra]
---

# Quotient vector spaces

Let $V$ be a vector space and $W \leq V$ a subspace.

Then the quotient $V/W$ is the set of cosets
\[
v + W.
\]

## Example

Take \(V = \mathbb{R}^2\) and \(W = \operatorname{span}(e_1)\).

Then \(V/W\) is one-dimensional.
